title: Project Overview
description: Start here to understand what voxlblade-builder is: a Svelte 5 + Vite + TypeScript damage calculator for the Roblox game Voxel Blade. Examine the README and package.json to learn the tech stack (Svelte 5 runes, Tailwind CSS v4, Vitest, pnpm) and the project's purpose.

title: Entry Point & Application Shell
description: See how the app boots: main.ts mounts the Svelte 5 app and initializes Vercel Analytics, while App.svelte is the monolithic root component (~3,978 lines) that manages all top-level state, modals, and child orchestration. This is the top of the dependency tree.

title: Foundation Types
description: Explore the shared type vocabulary that every other layer depends on. types.ts defines 20+ interfaces (StatMap, BuildState, Race, Guild, Armor, WeaponBlade, ProcCoefficient) and utility functions (applyUpgrade, canProc). dmgTypes.ts adds the damage computation result model (ComputedType, ComputedHit, BADGE_CONFIG).

title: Game Data Layer
description: Dive into the game's static data: JSON files for 11 entity datasets (armors, blades, handles, rings, runes, enchantments, races, guilds, perks) and TypeScript modules for mechanics definitions (weaponArts, Boost, BuffData, procCoefficients, weapon base damage). The engine/data/ sub-modules provide accessor functions that build lookup maps from JSON.

title: Game Constants & Tuning Parameters
description: Examine the pure-constant modules that parameterize every game mechanic: crit formulas, buff potency values, cooldown reduction, damage types, HP formulas, and storage keys. These are a centralized spreadsheet of game balance values with zero logic.

title: Calculation Engine
description: The core brain: build.ts orchestrates a pipeline (accumulateEquipment -> finalizePerks -> deriveResults) that transforms a BuildState into a complete BuildResult. Sub-modules handle weapon resolution, enchantment application, shrine bonuses, elemental damage bonuses, crit computation, and defense reduction.

title: State Management & Persistence
description: Learn how the Svelte store bridges the engine and UI. store.ts creates a writable store with debounced localStorage persistence and a derived store that runs calcBuild on every state change. Exports convenience functions (setEnchantment, swapEnchantments, clearBuild, moveArmorSlot) that encapsulate state mutation logic.

title: UI Design System (Primitives)
description: Explore the reusable UI building blocks: Badge, Button, Card, Divider, Panel, Section, and Stat components using Svelte 5 $props() runes. app.css defines the complete design token system (colors, spacing, radii, shadows, animations, Draconic theme variables). These have zero knowledge of game mechanics.

title: Feature UI Components
description: Domain-specific components that compose UI primitives with application logic: modal dialogs (RaceModal, GuildModal, WeaponPartModal, AccessorySelectModal), search/filter systems (StatFilter, WeaponStatFilter, TagFilter), and display components (WeaponStatsDisplay, CritIcon, DmgTotalTooltip). These bridge the design system and the application layer.

title: Damage Analysis Views
description: The largest components in the codebase: DamageAnalyzer.svelte (~6,700 lines) is the main analysis view with complex reactive computation chains, BaseDamageCalc.svelte (~2,566 lines) handles per-hit damage breakdowns, and CdrStepsCalc.svelte shows cooldown reduction details. These are the primary refactoring targets.
